title: quercus sessiliflora subsp. lanuginosa
intro: Quercus sessiliflora subsp. lanuginosa, also known as the woolly-leaved evergreen oak or woolly sessile oak, is a subspecies of evergreen oak tree native to the Iberian Peninsula. This plant has been found to have medicinal properties, particularly in its ability to act as an anti-inflammatory and antioxidant, making it a potential treatment for various health conditions. The health benefits of Quercus sessiliflora subsp. lanuginosa include reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health, as well as exhibiting antimicrobial and antifungal properties. Herbal preparations of this plant include tea, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to support overall health and well-being.
